title: Starbucks pilots TikTok program for boosting employee-generated content
tldr: Starbucks is launching a structured TikTok program to turn employees into content creators, formalizing employee-generated content as a brand channel. A concrete case study of EGC operationalized at scale.
why_fresh: new EGC program from a major brand
angle: How to build governance and creative freedom into an employee-generated content program without killing authenticity.

title: Fewer than one in 15 pieces of creator content delivers both audience engagement and ROI
tldr: New analysis finds the vast majority of creator content fails on either engagement or commercial return, challenging the creator-spend gold rush. A data-backed reality check on creator effectiveness.
angle: Why most creator content fails the ROI test — and what the rare winners do differently.

title: UK media consumption trends in 2026: Short-form vs. long-form video consumption insights
tldr: YouGov breaks down how UK audiences now split attention between short-form and long-form video. Direct evidence on shifting media-consumption habits.
angle: What the short vs long video split means for how brands should structure their content portfolios.

title: How Nation Media Group Built a Short-Video Newsroom That Competes With Entertainment
tldr: A detailed case study of how an African publisher rebuilt its newsroom around short-form video to win younger audiences. Concrete operational playbook for video-first publishing.
angle: What a short-video newsroom teaches brand publishers about competing for attention against entertainment.

title: These are the most important aspects of corporate affairs today
tldr: Deloitte's 2026 Corporate Affairs Report finds leaders prioritizing change management, employee belief, AI upskilling and political acumen — while media relations and purpose fade. A signal of where comms priorities are shifting.
angle: Why 'purpose' is out and 'employee belief' is in — what the new corporate affairs mandate means for content teams.

title: The demand for news video is growing (and that's a good thing for publishers)
tldr: Reuters Institute data shows genuine, sustained growth in demand for news video — distinct from the discredited 'pivot to video' of a decade ago. Reframes video as a real audience opportunity.
angle: How to read the new news-video demand without repeating the mistakes of the first pivot to video.

title: Inside the infrastructure behind Unilever's creator force
tldr: Unilever has built creator content into a full marketing operating mode, not a channel, after pledging half its media budget to social. A look at the systems behind creator-at-scale.
angle: What Unilever's creator infrastructure reveals about treating creators as a content operating system.

title: The Real Reason Personalization Fails: It Was Never The Editor's Job
tldr: Argues personalization keeps failing because publishers assigned it to editors instead of building it into systems and data ownership. A contrarian take on a persistent problem.
angle: Why personalization is an ops and data problem, not an editorial one — and who should actually own it.

title: 66% of social media users expect influencers they follow to participate in activism
tldr: A Sprout survey of 2,000 consumers finds most now expect influencers to take stands on social issues. Reshapes the calculus on brand-creator partnerships and risk.
angle: When silence becomes a liability: managing brand risk as audiences demand creator activism.

title: A 6-part AI search framework to make content more citation worthy
tldr: A practical GEO framework focused on clarity, credibility and third-party validation so AI tools find and cite your brand. Moves beyond keyword thinking for the AI-answer era.
angle: How content credibility — not keywords — becomes the new currency in AI search.

title: How Alex Cooper is Disrupting Traditional Media and Brand Storytelling
tldr: WSJ profiles how Alex Cooper built a media empire that's rewriting brand storytelling and the podcast-to-business pipeline. A case study in personality-led media businesses.
angle: What creator-led media empires teach legacy brands about owned audience and trust.

title: The "one big commercial" concept is dead
tldr: Fast Company argues the single hero ad is obsolete as fragmented attention forces brands toward modular, distributed content. A shift in how campaigns get built.
angle: From hero ad to modular content system: how to plan when one big spot no longer works.

title: Beehiiv's new Cloudflare partnership gives indie journalists a new level of control over AI crawlers
tldr: Beehiiv and Cloudflare are giving independent newsletter publishers tools to block AI crawlers previously only available to big publishers. Levels the playing field on content rights.
angle: What AI-crawler control means for newsletter creators weighing visibility against ownership.

title: Why "AI Productivity Gains" Is the Wrong Pitch for Every Stakeholder Above You
tldr: Argues productivity framing won't move CMOs, CFOs or legal — each needs the AI value reframed in their own metric. A practical internal-persuasion guide for content leaders.
angle: How to translate AI's content value into the language each executive stakeholder actually buys.

title: Collaborative Posts Are Coming to LinkedIn — What They Are and How to Make Them Work
tldr: LinkedIn just launched Collaborative Posts, a new co-authored format with implications for reach and B2B thought leadership. Early look at a new platform feature.
angle: How LinkedIn Collaborative Posts could reshape B2B thought-leadership and co-marketing.
